headline: Stock Market Today, July 6: Southeast Asian Mobility Upstart Grab Falls as Uber CEO Exits Board
summary: On July 6, 2026, the ride-hailing super-app's stock dropped 1.28% after Dara Khosrowshahi stepped down from its board.

raw.headline: Stock Market Today, July 6: Southeast Asian Mobility Upstart Grab Falls as Uber CEO Exits Board
raw.summary: On July 6, 2026, the ride-hailing super-app's stock dropped 1.28% after Dara Khosrowshahi stepped down from its board.

headline: DoorDash (DASH) Stock Looks Strong On Returns But Rich On Earnings
summary: DoorDash stock has delivered a very strong 147.4% return over the past three years, yet the broader valuation checks now lean toward the shares looking expensive rather than like a clear bargain. Over three years, DoorDash has returned 147.4%, which puts recent short-term weakness in context and raises the bar for what future performance would need to justify today’s price. Recent headlines around expanding beyond food delivery and growing subscription and grocery volumes can support...

headline: DoorDash to Announce Second Quarter 2026 Results on August 5, 2026
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, July 06, 2026--DoorDash, Inc. (NASDAQ: DASH) today announced that the company’s second quarter 2026 financial results will be released after the U.S. financial markets close on Wednesday, August 5, 2026. The company’s earnings press release will be made available on the DoorDash Investor Relations website at ir.doordash.com.

headline: 2 of Wall Street’s Favorite Stocks to Own for Decades and 1 That Underwhelm
summary: The stocks in this article have caught Wall Street’s attention in a big way, with price targets implying returns above 20%. But investors should take these forecasts with a grain of salt because analysts typically say nice things about companies so their firms can win business in other product lines like M&A advisory.

headline: Wells Fargo Maintains Equal-Weight on DoorDash, Lowers Price Target to $199
summary: Wells Fargo  analyst Ken Gawrelski   maintains DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) with a Equal-Weight and lowers the price target from $200 to $199.
